description_long: This is a tall, leafless deciduous tree with a thick, gnarled trunk and numerous long, twisted branches extending outward in various directions. The bark appears rough and dark, with deep grooves and a weathered texture, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The branches are irregularly spaced and forked, creating a dramatic, skeletal silhouette against the sky. There are no visible leaves or foliage, giving the tree a stark, barren appearance typical of winter or a dead specimen. The base of the trunk is broad and slightly flared, anchoring the tree firmly to the ground. This type of tree is commonly found in urban parks, streetscapes, or as a naturalistic feature in landscaped environments.
description: A tall, leafless tree with a thick, rough trunk and many twisted, gnarled branches. Its dark, weathered bark and dramatic, skeletal form give it a striking, barren appearance.
description_view_0: The tree is seen from an angle highlighting its thick trunk and two main branches splitting off, with several smaller, twisted branches extending outward.
description_view_1: This view shows the tree from the side, emphasizing the vertical alignment of the trunk and the irregular, sparse branching pattern.
description_view_2: The tree is viewed from the opposite side, revealing the full spread of its longest branches and the pronounced curvature of the trunk.
description_view_3: A rear view displays the trunk's width and the symmetrical, forked arrangement of the upper branches, with fewer branches visible on the left.
